findings.issue: Report prose shown on pages is often mathematically garbled due lossy extraction, which harms comprehension.
findings.evidence: render-pages.tsx renders meta.summary/narrative directly (line 129, 133-141). Example from site/public/data/v1/reports/ring_lazy_jump_ext/meta.json: "N=100,q=2/3, =1 and only varied..."; from ring_lazy_jump_ext_rev2/meta.json: "K \\ 2,4\\ , q=2/3 , =0.01...".
findings.fix: Harden TeX->text pipeline: preserve inline math tokens, convert to KaTeX-safe inline spans, and add CI checks for corruption patterns (e.g., ', =0', 't_ p1', raw '\\ ').

findings.issue: Asset list rendering can collide on React keys because key uses only sha256 while duplicate hashes exist in a single report.
findings.evidence: render-pages.tsx line 243 uses key={asset.sha256}. In ring_lazy_jump_ext/meta.json and ring_lazy_jump/meta.json, first 20 assets already contain repeated sha256 values (same content in multiple paths).
findings.fix: Use stable unique key `${asset.sha256}:${asset.web_path}` and optionally deduplicate assets by (sha256, web_path label policy) before rendering.

findings.issue: Log-scale mode can drop a large fraction of points, risking misleading interpretation.
findings.evidence: ReportPlotPanel.tsx lines 84-97 replace non-positive y with null and only show aggregate dropped count. Example dataset site/public/data/v1/reports/grid2d_rect_bimodality/series/ot_scan_bias2d-2.json has 60% non-positive values.
findings.fix: Warn per series with dropped percentage, disable log toggle when drop ratio is high, or offer symlog/offset transform as safer alternative.

findings.issue: Theory-map QA check for duplicate findings is a false negative, so coherence diagnostics are over-optimistic.
findings.evidence: theory_map.json has consistency_checks entry {check:"duplicate_key_findings", pass:true, details:[]}, but exact duplicates exist across reports (e.g., grid2d_blackboard_bimodality and grid2d_reflecting_bimodality share 3 identical key_findings strings).
findings.fix: Recompute duplicate checks across all report metas with normalization (trim/markdown-strip/punctuation-fold) and include offending report_ids/details in theory_map output.

findings.issue: Per-report freshness signal is weak: all reports share the same updated_at timestamp.
findings.evidence: site/public/data/v1/index.json has one identical updated_at for all 14 reports (same as generated_at).
findings.fix: Store true per-report source mtime or commit timestamp; keep generated_at as pipeline build time only.

findings.issue: Math-card rendering logic is duplicated in two branches, increasing maintenance drift risk.
findings.evidence: render-pages.tsx lines 162-178 and 184-200 repeat near-identical JSX for math blocks.
findings.fix: Extract a reusable MathBlockCard component and map once (with optional collapsed pagination).

quick_wins: Change asset key to sha256+web_path (1-line UI fix with immediate collision reduction).
Add CI lint rule for extracted text corruption tokens before publishing meta.json.
Show dropped-per-series badge in log mode and auto-fallback to linear when all points vanish.
Upgrade theory_map duplicate check to emit concrete duplicates (report_id + text hash).
Backfill index.json updated_at from report-level mtimes/git metadata.

crosscheck.notes: Directly renders extracted summary/narrative; asset key uses sha256 only; duplicated math-card JSX blocks.
Good controls and fetch abort handling; log transform drops non-positive data aggressively with limited guidance.
Structure valid (14 reports, groups present), but updated_at lacks per-report granularity.
Card counts and report mapping are internally consistent, but duplicate_key_findings check misses real duplicates.
All index report_ids are mapped in theory cards; no unmapped/extra IDs.
